title: elaeocarpus surigaensis
intro: Elaeocarpus surigaensis, also known as the Mindanao blueberry tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae, native to the Philippines. The leaves and fruits of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. Elaeocarpus surigaensis is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. The plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal purposes, and its fruits can be consumed fresh or used as a natural food coloring.
